Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Erdi Syla
Message:
Feature: end-to-end Keycloak signup, hosted login, and anonymous invite acceptance (#125)

* Feature: end-to-end Keycloak signup, hosted login, and anonymous invite acceptance

  Closes the account-creation gap: landlords self-register, anyone logs in via
  the Keycloak hosted page, and tenants onboard through anonymous invite
  acceptance (token is the auth).

  Backend
  - Add IUserAccountProvisioner + KeycloakAdminClient (Admin REST: create user →
    reset-password → assign realm role) with a client-credentials token cache and
    standard resilience handler; 409 → UserAlreadyExistsException (mapped to 409).
  - Add POST /api/v1/auth/register-landlord (anonymous, rate-limited); User row
    created lazily on first /me call.
  - Rewrite invite acceptance as anonymous (POST /invites/{token}/accept): create
    Keycloak user (Tenant role) + User + Lease and mark Accepted in one unit of
    work; reject already-registered emails with 409. Account is bound to
    invite.Email (acceptor supplies no email).
  - Set TokenValidationParameters.ValidIssuer = Authority so the
    Authority/MetadataAddress split validates browser-issued tokens.

  Frontend
  - /login is now a hosted-login button (login() self-initializes Keycloak before
    redirecting); /signup posts to register-landlord; delete the old
    email/password login form, mutation, and schema.
  - keycloak.ts uses check-sso + silent-check-sso.html; invite wizard collects
    first/last/password and posts the new body shape.
  - Middleware: /login, /signup, /logout are public.

  Infra
  - Enable service accounts on myproperty-api (realm-management roles); disable
    Direct Access Grant on myproperty-frontend; add verify-realm-import.sh + CI.

  Docs: docs/milestones/m5-auth-end-to-end.md.

* Fix: dispose FormUrlEncodedContent in KeycloakAdminTokenCache

   On .NET Core 3.0+ HttpClient no longer disposes request content, so the
   FormUrlEncodedContent built in FetchTokenAsync was left to the GC. Switch
   to  so it is disposed deterministically, including on the
   exception path.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: drinprekaj
Message:
Feature: SQL optimization proof — 3 EXPLAIN ANALYZE queries (M3.4) (#38)

Summary

    Profiled three real-workload queries with EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) against a seeded 200k-payment dataset;
    recorded warm-cache before/after timings (committed results.txt for audit).
    Q2 finding: the original full-column IX_payments_DueDate was worse than no index for the recurring
    overdue-scan job — DueDate < today matches ~74% of rows. Replaced with a partial index
    IX_payments_DueDate_Outstanding scoped to Status = 'Outstanding' AND DeletedAt IS NULL. ~103× speedup   on that job and ~200× faster than the naive index it replaces.
    Q1 (landlord upcoming-payments) and Q3 (invite-by-token) confirmed the existing composite + unique indexes earn their keep (~22× and ~13× respectively); no schema changes needed.
    Flipped M3.4 to ✅ done in the milestone tracker. Updated backend/CLAUDE.md index list to specify the partial
    scope.
